chunks.text: The relationship between politics and money has been, across time and place
an eternal issue of democracy.
A politician swears to represent the will of the voters.
But in reality, they are bound by the source of campaign funds.
Whether democrat or republican, political activity costs large sums of money.
Campaigns, advertising, staff salaries.
Where to obtain these funds becomes the issue.
Corporate donations have long been a subject of dispute.
If a company grants money, it expects policies favorable to that company.
To become a supporter of a corporation carries an implied expectation.
Cases of corruption and bribery have brought down many minister and lawmakers.
The anger of the public demands strict punishment for corrupt politicians.
Countries implement political fund laws.
A maximum ceiling on donations, the duty of disclosure, examination of how money is spent.
But methods that imitate and evade the regulatory net keep being developed.
Political fund organizations, fundraising parties, loopholes are many.
To ensure true democracy, the setting up of institutions alone is inadequate.
The watch of citizens, the investigator reporting of the media
and the involvement of voters are indispensable.
For one's vote to carry meaning as a voter
the responsibility to watch politics must be fulfilled.
